title: Substance Use Disorder Referrals * Medicaid Recipients - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Substance Use Disorder Referrals * Medicaid Recipients 18 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Medicaid Recipients Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ADVANCED HEALTH COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Advanced Health is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Advanced Health Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 269-7400 Visit Website More Details ADVANCED HEALTH FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 269-7400 Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH BROOKINGS OFFICE COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 471-4106 Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH MEDFORD OFFICE COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF AllCare CCO coordinates health insurance services for Medicaid members assigned to its Coordinated Care Organization (CCO). Includes specialized information and referrals to healthcare providers for medical, dental, and behavioral health. Additional wellness benefits for AllCare CCO members include medical transportation, pain management, weight management programs, and a 24/7 nurse help line. AllCare CCO members can receive vouchers from providers for taking healthy actions such as attending appointments and tobacco cessation, to purchase items at the BABE Store such as diapers, books, and blankets. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (888) 460-0185 Visit Website More Details CAREOREGON COORDINATED HEALTH CARE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF CareOregon is a Managed Care Organization (MCO) that coordinates Medicaid services for Coordinated Care Organizations (CCO) Jackson Care Connect and Columbia Pacific. CareOregon also provides health insurance services through its partnering CCO, Health Share. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, substance use disorder treatment, and non-emergency transportation for health care visits covered by the CCO. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 416-4100 Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Eastern Oregon Coordinated Care Organization (EOCCO) is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Eastern Oregon Coordinated Care Organization Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (503) 765-3521 Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (for individuals being discharged from the hospital or who have a set move-in date for a new home and need a temporary place to stay before then) -Electronics such as cell phones, computers, and other communication devices when there are no state programs available in the area -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships or home equipment -Beds and mattresses for children under 21 or for adults with a diagnosis of a back or spine condition -Car seats if there are no other county or programs available in the area All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Visit website to review additional eligibility criteria regarding specific programs and benefits. Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (503) 765-3521 Visit Website More Details HEALTH SHARE OF OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Health Share of Oregon is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Health Share of Oregon; members are able to choose medical care through CareOregon, Kaiser Permanente, Legacy Health, OHSU, or Providence. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:45pm Email (503) 416-8090 Visit Website More Details INFORMATIONAL RECORD COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) is a local Oregon Health Plan (OHP) or (Medicaid) plan that helps OHP members use their benefits. CCOs are made up of all types of healthcare providers in a community. They work together to care for OHP members in an area or region of the state. If your caller has OHP, remember to suggest contacting their CCO to find out which providers or services are covered under their plan. Confidential Address Eligibility: Oregon Health Plan recipients Hours: Not applicable Visit Website More Details INTERCOMMUNITY HEALTH NETWORK CCO COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF InterCommunity Health Network is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify.
